plans.title: Muni five-layer enactment for .project/
plans.description: Restructure .project/ from flat-block storage into a layered model where each layer owns its own findings, decisions, and lifecycle. Enacted by hand initially as the test case for what pi-project must eventually generate natively. The hand-enactment itself reveals the framework gaps (captured in framework-gaps.json) that the consumer migration arc must address for the layered structure to become expressible as configuration rather than manual scaffolding.

layers.name: Identity
layers.purpose: What this project is, who it is for, what cannot change. Stable across the project lifetime. Foundational and rarely revised.
layers.canonical_vocabulary: Project Charter + README + Glossary + Coding Standards
layers.ownership_principle: Changes require user authority and affect every downstream layer

layers.name: Specification
layers.purpose: What the system must do and how it is structured. Evolves with design decisions. Architectural contradictions and missing requirements are owned here.
layers.ownership_principle: Findings discovered during design review of a spec live attached to that spec, not in global issues. decisions record each design decision with a lifecycle gate for user-authored transitions to 'enacted'.

target_blocks.name: requirements.json
target_blocks.notes: unchanged in shape; may embed findings later

target_blocks.name: architecture.json
target_blocks.notes: unchanged in shape; may embed findings later

target_blocks.name: decisions.json
target_blocks.notes: live-decision block with open/enacted lifecycle; entries persist through enactment; each decision must cite its forcing issue/gap/feature

target_blocks.name: spec-reviews.json
target_blocks.notes: per-target design reviews with embedded findings arrays — new for this plan

target_blocks.name: framework-gaps.json
target_blocks.notes: L2 capability gaps in the framework itself — new for this plan

target_blocks.name: layer-plans.json
target_blocks.notes: this file; L2 restructure plans — new for this plan

layers.name: Work
layers.purpose: What is planned and in what order. Hierarchical decomposition — roadmap → milestone → epic → story → task. Each feature/epic owns its own scope, acceptance criteria, and scoped finding registry.
layers.ownership_principle: A task belongs to a story, a story to an epic, an epic to a milestone. Findings discovered while working on a story live in that story's feature node, not in the global tracker. Global task lists are materialized views, not the authoritative store.

target_blocks.name: features.json
target_blocks.notes: seed: one feature per epic, with embedded stories and tasks; hand-enactment of the nested structure until FGAP-001 lands

target_blocks.name: features/
target_blocks.notes: target: once FGAP-001 lands, each feature becomes features/{id}.json and stories/tasks become subfiles

layers.name: Execution
layers.purpose: What is currently true. Changes per session. Done, broken, in-progress, verified. Implementation defects and regressions are owned here.
layers.canonical_vocabulary: Iteration state + V&V records + session notes
layers.ownership_principle: Per-task verification records live attached to the task. Session handoff records live attached to the active feature. Defects discovered during execution are owned by the story or task that was in-flight.

target_blocks.name: handoff.json
target_blocks.notes: session notes; schema exists, created on demand

target_blocks.name: issues_view.json
target_blocks.notes: target: derived index over scope-owned finding registries once FGAP-002/003 land; current issues.json migrates into scope-owned arrays

layers.name: Memory
layers.purpose: What has been learned through incidents. Accumulates, never shrinks. Rules derived from past failures.
layers.canonical_vocabulary: Runbook + Postmortem archive + Lessons learned + Knowledge base
layers.ownership_principle: Each entry is tied to an originating incident. No speculative rules. Entries cross-reference the features/tasks/findings they derived from.

target_blocks.name: feedback-memory.json
target_blocks.notes: target: canonicalize the ~/.claude/.../memory/feedback_*.md corpus as a queryable block with incident cross-references

target_blocks.name: postmortems.json
target_blocks.notes: target: structured postmortem records with cross-references to the incidents they analyze

migration_phases.name: New L2 schemas in place
migration_phases.description: Create the five new schemas (adrs, spec-reviews, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans) in .project/schemas/. Existing schemas untouched.
exit_criteria: .project/schemas/decisions.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/spec-reviews.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/features.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/framework-gaps.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07
.project/schemas/layer-plans.schema.json exists and is valid draft-07

migration_phases.name: Seed initial L2/L3 artifacts
migration_phases.description: Populate each new block with seed data representing the current state of the consumer migration arc — three open decisions, one spec-review target, one feature with story decomposition, six framework gaps, one layer plan.
exit_criteria: decisions.json contains DEC-0001 through DEC-0003 in proposed state
spec-reviews.json contains REVIEW-001 in not-started state
features.json contains FEAT-001 (consumer migration epic) in proposed state
framework-gaps.json contains FGAP-001 through FGAP-006 in identified state
layer-plans.json contains PLAN-001 in draft state
every data file validates against its schema via AJV

migration_phases.name: Run design review of jit-agents-spec.md
migration_phases.description: Spawn a fresh-context independent reviewer subagent against docs/planning/jit-agents-spec.md with the scope declared in REVIEW-001. Findings populate REVIEW-001.findings and drive DEC-0001/0002/0003 state from proposed toward decided where evidence supports it.
exit_criteria: REVIEW-001 advances to complete
Every finding in REVIEW-001.findings reaches a terminal state (resolved/wontfix/duplicate)
Findings that generate new decisions are linked via produces_decision
REVIEW-001.clean is true

migration_phases.name: User-authored transitions on gating decisions
migration_phases.description: User reviews DEC-0001/0002/0003 in light of the design-review findings and advances each to enacted or requests revision. All open-to-enacted transitions require user authority per the lifecycle metadata. Until each gating decision is enacted, FEAT-001 cannot advance from proposed to approved.
exit_criteria: DEC-0001 in enacted state with enacted_by=user
DEC-0002 in enacted state with enacted_by=user
DEC-0003 in enacted state with enacted_by=user

migration_phases.name: FEAT-001 story execution
migration_phases.description: User advances FEAT-001 to approved. Each story within FEAT-001 executes in dependency order, starting with the decision-enacted boundary changes (STORY-001/002/003 for model-pin, thinking-seam, parseModelSpec move) and proceeding to the consumer call-site migration (STORY-004/005), bundled YAML updates (STORY-006), duplicate removal (STORY-007), empirical verification (STORY-008), and issue closeout (STORY-009).
exit_criteria: FEAT-001 in complete state
All nine stories in FEAT-001.stories in complete state
The five existing issues named in blocks_resolved are closed in issues.json or migrated to their scope-owned registries
pi-workflows and pi-behavior-monitors import agent infrastructure from pi-jit-agents

migration_phases.name: Retroactive issue migration
migration_phases.description: Migrate existing issues.json entries into scope-owned finding registries. Each issue gets a layer tag (L2/L3/L4) and is moved to the appropriate owner — spec-reviews for L2 design issues, features for L3 scope conflicts, features[].findings for L4 implementation defects. Global issues.json becomes a derived view. Depends on FGAP-002 and FGAP-003 landing for proper enforcement, but can be hand-enacted in the interim.
exit_criteria: Every issue in issues.json has a layer tag or has been moved to a scope-owned registry
issues.json is either empty or contains only entries not yet classified

migration_phases.name: Framework gap closure
migration_phases.description: Address the six framework gaps (FGAP-001 through FGAP-006) so the hand-enacted L1–L5 structure becomes natively expressible. Each gap becomes a feature of its own with stories and tasks. Order is informed by priority (P1 first) and by what the remaining phases need.
exit_criteria: FGAP-001 through FGAP-006 all in closed or wontfix state
pi-project natively supports nested blocks, scoped findings, materialized views, authorship attestation, state-machine validation, and schema versioning
produces: framework substrate for the layered model
